title: mentha viridis var. walteriana
intro: Mentha viridis var. walteriana, also known as American peppermint, is a variety of peppermint that is native to North America. It is a hardy perennial herb with fragrant, dark green leaves and purple flowers, and is commonly used for its medicinal and culinary properties. American peppermint has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been found to have various health benefits, including relieving digestive issues, reducing nausea and headaches, and supporting respiratory health. American peppermint can be consumed as a tea, added to salads, or used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and essential oils.
